data.narrative:
Smack Technologies is a Series A defense and security company founded by Andy Markoff (former MARSOC officer and Palantir veteran) and Clint Alanis (20+ years Marine special operations). The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $32M total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, yielding $64M. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M)—produces a median valuation of $225M, with a range of $125M to $500M.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $54M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $144M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Smack should be valued closer to early-stage raised capital benchmarks or to the significantly higher median of peer companies in this sector. Key downside risks include execution risk at Series A and competitive positioning relative to well-capitalized peers like Anduril. Upside hinges on management pedigree and defensible IP differentiation within the crowded defense tech market.
